title: Just watched Evangelion Death
wikitext:
[]: Just watched Evangelion: Death.             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sat Jan 31, 2015 5:26 pm 1. It's ridiculous just how much better this dub is than the dub for NGE. It's just incredible. Why is it so much better? Did the actors become more experienced? Did they become more acquainted with their characters? Or was ADV just much, much worse at directing VAs than Manga Entertainment? Either way, a redub of this caliber would be the greatest thing eve-- eh, screw hyperbole, it'd be the greatest thing in two-to-three years.. on home video. 2. Were the scenes in this movie that were taken from NGE literally taken directly from NGE, or were they reanimated from scratch? I know they were redubbed (see above), but I'm not sure about the animation.

[]:             Quote Post by Rosenakahara » Sat Jan 31, 2015 5:34 pm the dub gets better here..........then so, so SO much worse for EoE, ritsuko just sounds hilariously bad and the guy who plays fuyutsuki isn't even trying.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 31, 2015 5:47 pm The more obsessive members will be able to give exact details of the new animation; but some the recap material is quite blatantly not just a clip from the broadcast version (like Rei's smile after Operation Yashima).

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jan 31, 2015 7:51 pm I love watching Death right before revising The End of Evangelion. It’s a great way to enjoy Eva without having to sit through the entire TV series. Some of the scenes in Death were reanimated, like tines said Rei’s smile. Both the Japanese and the English dubs were re-recorded for that film. I love how that movie recaps everything thematically rather than chronologically just before hitting us with EoE. It truly is amazing.

[]: Re: Just watched Evangelion: Death.             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jan 31, 2015 8:35 pm Tumbling Down wrote: Or was ADV just much, much worse at directing VAs than Manga Entertainment?

[]: Re: Just watched Evangelion: Death.             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Feb 12, 2015 4:59 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: First off, Manga Entertainment didn't do the dub production, they just distributed. The dub work was done through Gaijin Productions, which was Amanda Winn and Jason C. Lee, who were voice actors for ADV, Rei Ayanami and Shigeru Aoba, respectively. Amanda Winn Lee supposedly did some voice direction, possibly uncredited, towards the end of the series. Secondly, it's a little unfair to compare, because ADV did their dubbing back in the dark ages when they were paying for studio time at an hourly rate. They had time limitations, technology limitations, whereas Gaijin had more time and ability to finesse things. ADV had to throw away perfectly good takes because they came out distorted, for example. And you could say that the actors had lived with the characters and had improved over time.

[]: Re: Just watched Evangelion: Death.             Quote Post by BC Baron » Tue Feb 17, 2015 10:12 pm Tumbling Down wrote: If ADV knew their dub wasn't as good as it could've been, why didn't they try again for the mid-2000s re-release?

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Feb 17, 2015 10:41 pm They did. They replaced Aobas old VA with Vic Mignogna.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Feb 17, 2015 11:34 pm Tumbling Down wrote: ADV knew their dub wasn't as good as it could've been, why didn't they try again for the mid-2000s re-release? The (admittedly incompetent) businessman in me says that would've been a great way to make people who owned the previous set buy the new one.
